meta.project: Reclaim - AI-assisted expense reimbursement with intelligent decision support

product_overview: Reclaim is an AI-assisted expense reimbursement platform that uses multimodal OCR and a LangGraph-orchestrated compliance agent to pre-screen claims, surface exceptions to HR, and maintain a permanent audit trail. It speeds reviews, enforces policy consistency, and preserves human final-decision authority.
core_goals: Reduce HR manual review workload by pre-classifying claims and surfacing exceptions (Efficiency by Exception).
Ensure consistent, auditable policy enforcement by extracting policy conditions into structured JSON and injecting them into evaluation (deterministic compliance).
Provide fast, accurate OCR extraction of receipts and record any employee edits as immutable audit entries (Fraud Trap).
Deliver transparent AI reasoning (per-receipt verdicts, confidence scores, and explicit policy citations) while keeping HR as the final decision authority.
Enforce role-based access control and strict data isolation for secure operations.

features.name: User Login
features.description: Email/password authentication with role-aware behavior (HR vs Employee). Backend endpoint: POST /api/v1/auth/login (see codebase route app/login/page.tsx). The login flow issues JWT bearer tokens used to access protected APIs; invalid credentials return 401. See PRD overview for portal toggle and demo-login behavior (page 1 and app/login/page.tsx).
user_flows: POST /api/v1/auth/login with valid credentials -> Receive 200 with JWT token and role=HR -> GET /api/v1/reimbursements/ with JWT -> Receive 200 with claims list (HR dashboard data)
POST /api/v1/auth/login with invalid credentials -> Receive 401 Unauthorized with error message

features.name: WF1 - Policy Document Upload (Policy Sync)
features.description: HR uploads a policy PDF/DOCX which the backend parses (LangChain/LangGraph pipeline) and stores structured policy conditions and embeddings (policy upload is a real backend call; editing existing mock policies may be local-only per known limitations). See Policy Studio details on pages 10-11.
user_flows: POST /api/v1/policies/upload with JWT and multipart/form-data (policy PDF/DOCX) -> Receive 200 with policy_id and status: processing -> GET /api/v1/policies/ with JWT -> Receive 200 including new policy (becomes ACTIVE after processing)
POST /api/v1/policies/upload with unsupported file type or oversized file (>10MB) with JWT -> Receive 415 Unsupported Media Type or 413 Payload Too Large -> Frontend shows upload error banner

features.name: WF1 - Policy List & Management
features.description: HR can list, filter, and delete policies via GET /api/v1/policies/ and DELETE /api/v1/policies/{policy_id}. Note: in the MVP some edits are local-only (see known limitations). See Policy Studio flow on pages 10-11 and the policy lifecycle description (archive/activate).
user_flows: GET /api/v1/policies/ with JWT -> Receive 200 with policies array -> DELETE /api/v1/policies/{policy_id} with JWT -> Receive 204 No Content -> GET /api/v1/policies/ with JWT -> Receive 200 with updated list (deleted policy absent)
DELETE /api/v1/policies/{nonexistent_policy_id} with JWT -> Receive 404 Not Found

features.name: WF2 - Employee Receipt Upload & OCR Extraction
features.description: Employees upload receipt images or PDFs; the Document Agent extracts structured fields (merchant, date, amount, currency, category, confidence). Files are uploaded to POST /api/v1/documents/upload and claims are submitted to POST /api/v1/reimbursements/. The side-by-side verification (Fraud Trap) records any edits as an immutable change_summary (pages 10, 18).
user_flows: POST /api/v1/documents/upload with JWT and receipt file (JPEG/PNG/PDF) -> Receive 200 with document_id and extracted_fields (merchant, amount, date, category, confidence) -> POST /api/v1/reimbursements/ with JWT and claim payload referencing document_id(s) -> Receive 201 with reimbursement_id and status: PENDING_REVIEW (AI pre-screen queued/completed)
POST /api/v1/documents/upload with JWT and unsupported file type (e.g., .exe) -> Receive 415 Unsupported Media Type

features.name: WF3 - Compliance & Fraud Detection (HR Review)
features.description: HR reviews flagged reimbursements. The Compliance Agent produces per-receipt verdicts and an overall judgment. HR fetches reimbursement detail via GET /api/v1/reimbursements/{reim_id} and updates status via PATCH /api/v1/reimbursements/{reim_id}/status. The audit trail includes AI extraction, employee edits, risk level, and per-receipt notes (pages 11-19).
user_flows: GET /api/v1/reimbursements/{reim_id} with HR JWT -> Receive 200 with full reimbursement record (per-receipt verdicts, AI judgment, confidence, change_summary) -> PATCH /api/v1/reimbursements/{reim_id}/status with HR JWT and body {action: 'APPROVE', hr_note: 'Approved after review'} -> Receive 200 with updated status APPROVED
PATCH /api/v1/reimbursements/{reim_id}/status with JWT of non-HR user -> Receive 403 Forbidden

features.name: HR Dashboard - Claims Triage
features.description: HR views pre-bucketed claims (Passed AI Review vs Requires Attention) via GET /api/v1/reimbursements/ with query/filters. Dashboard supports search and filtering; clicking a claim loads the detailed claim via GET /api/v1/reimbursements/{reim_id}. See triage bucket descriptions on pages 11-12 and 18-19.
user_flows: GET /api/v1/reimbursements/?filter=requires_attention with HR JWT -> Receive 200 with filtered claims list -> GET /api/v1/reimbursements/{reim_id} with HR JWT -> Receive 200 with claim detail (evidence panel)
GET /api/v1/reimbursements/ without JWT -> Receive 401 Unauthorized

features.name: Form Validation - Login
features.description: Client-side Zod + React Hook Form validation on the login form prevents invalid submissions (empty fields, invalid email format). If validation passes, the app calls POST /api/v1/auth/login. See app/login/page.tsx and validation notes in the codebase.
user_flows: Fill email and password valid per Zod schema -> POST /api/v1/auth/login with credentials -> Receive 200 with JWT token
Attempt to submit login form with invalid email format -> Frontend Zod validation blocks submission -> No API call made (inline validation error shown)

features.name: File Upload Validation
features.description: Uploads enforce allowed file types and size limits (UI states max 10MB; backend enforces constraints). Policy upload uses POST /api/v1/policies/upload; document upload uses POST /api/v1/documents/upload. See file validation rules on app/hr/policy/page.tsx and known constraints in the PRD.
user_flows: POST /api/v1/policies/upload with JWT and valid PDF <=10MB -> Receive 200 with policy_id and processing status
POST /api/v1/policies/upload with JWT and file >10MB -> Receive 413 Payload Too Large

features.name: Security & Gating
features.description: JWT bearer authentication and role-based access control protect APIs. HR-only endpoints reject employee tokens. Unauthenticated requests receive 401; unauthorized role requests receive 403. See middleware.ts and deps-based RBAC in the codebase and PRD section on access control (page 22).
user_flows: POST /api/v1/auth/login with valid HR credentials -> Receive 200 with JWT -> GET /api/v1/policies/ with HR JWT -> Receive 200 with policies (authorized access)
GET /api/v1/policies/ with Employee JWT (employee role) -> Receive 403 Forbidden

features.name: Stateful Audit Trail & Claim History
features.description: Employees and HR can retrieve historical claim records and full chain-of-evidence via GET /api/v1/reimbursements/ and GET /api/v1/reimbursements/{reim_id}. Each reimbursement record includes per-receipt AI extraction, change_summary JSONB, human_edited flag, risk severity, verdicts, and final decisions (pages 12, 17, 21).
user_flows: GET /api/v1/reimbursements/?user_id=self with Employee JWT -> Receive 200 with employee's reimbursements -> GET /api/v1/reimbursements/{reim_id} with Employee JWT -> Receive 200 with full chain-of-evidence (AI extraction, edits, verdicts)
GET /api/v1/reimbursements/{other_user_reim_id} with Employee JWT -> Receive 403 Forbidden

routes.description: Employee and HR login page with portal toggle, email/password form, demo login buttons
HR dashboard showing pending and approved reimbursement claims, KPI cards, filterable table
HR Policy Studio - upload, view, edit and delete policy documents (WF1)
HR claim review page for approving/rejecting a reimbursement bundle (WF3)
HR read-only view of a passed-AI-review claim bundle
HR history of all past processed claims
Employee dashboard showing personal claim summary
Employee submit page (WF2) - receipt upload, OCR extraction, claim submission
Employee list of own submitted claims

features.name: User Login
features.description: Login with email and password credentials with role-based redirection (HR to /hr/dashboard, Employee to /employee/submit)
user_interactions: Click Employee Portal or HR Dashboard tab toggle
Fill email input field with valid email
Fill password input field
Click Sign In button
Click Employee Demo or Admin Demo quick-login buttons
Click Forgot Password? button (shows MVP restriction toast)
See error message on invalid credentials
See loading spinner while login is in progress

features.name: WF1 - Policy Document Upload (Policy Sync)
features.description: HR admin uploads a policy PDF or DOCX, backend parses it with LangChain AI, stores embeddings in pgvector, and the policy appears in the library
user_interactions: Click + New Policy button to open edit view
Fill Policy Name field
Fill Department field
Click Upload Policy Document drop zone or drag a PDF/DOCX file
Optionally add appendix files via Add Source button
Click Save button
See animated processing screen (Uploading -> Analyzing -> Saving steps)
See updated policy list after successful upload
See error banner if upload fails

features.name: WF1 - Policy List & Management
features.description: HR can view, filter, edit and delete policies in the Policy Studio
user_interactions: Filter policies by status (Active, Impending, Expired)
Search policies by name
Click a policy card to open edit view
Edit policy name, department, version, effective date
Change policy status via dropdown
Click Delete button and confirm deletion in modal
Click Back to Policy Studio to return to list

features.name: WF2 - Employee Receipt Upload & OCR Extraction
features.description: Employee uploads receipt images, the backend OCR extracts amount/date/category, and the employee submits the claim
user_interactions: Navigate to employee submit page after login
Upload receipt image (JPEG, PNG, PDF)
See OCR-extracted fields (merchant, amount, date, category)
Review and potentially edit OCR fields (discrepancy creation)
Fill in trip purpose and destination
Click Submit Claim button
See success or error confirmation

features.name: WF3 - Compliance & Fraud Detection (HR Review)
features.description: HR reviews flagged reimbursements where OCR amount differs from user-input amount; AI evaluates compliance against policy RAG
user_interactions: Navigate to a claim flagged as Policy Flagged or Awaiting Review
View AI judgment, confidence score, and summary
View individual line items with claimed vs approved amounts
View audit notes per line item
Edit approved amounts for line items (manual override)
Click Approve, Partial Approve, or Reject action button
Enter HR note on rejection/approval
See confirmation after status update

features.name: HR Dashboard - Claims Triage
features.description: HR views pending and approved claims in a filterable table with search capability
user_interactions: Switch between Requires Attention and Passed AI Review tabs
Search claims by employee name, category or status
Open filter panel and filter by AI status, category, and amount range
Click a claim row to navigate to review or view page
Click View All Pending Requests to open modal with full list

features.name: Form Validation - Login
features.description: Zod-based form validation on login page with inline error messages
user_interactions: Submit login form with empty email field and see validation error
Submit login form with invalid email format and see error message
Submit login form with empty password and see validation error
Submit with wrong credentials and see server error banner

features.name: File Upload Validation
features.description: Policy upload enforces file type and size constraints (PDF/DOCX/TXT, max 10MB stated in UI)
user_interactions: Attempt to upload an oversized file (>5MB) and see rejection or warning
Attempt to upload an unsupported file type and see rejection

features.name: Security & Gating
features.description: Route guards blocking unauthenticated and unauthorized access
user_interactions: Navigate to /hr/dashboard without session and get redirected to login
Authenticated employee navigates to /hr/dashboard and gets blocked
Log out and attempt to access protected routes

features.name: Stateful Audit Trail & Claim History
features.description: Employees and HR can view historical claims with full chain-of-evidence
user_interactions: Open employee claims list and click a claim
View chain-of-evidence (original extraction, edits, verdicts, HR decision)
HR searches processed claims in history
HR opens a past claim and accesses export settlement form option

known_limitations.issue: Employee submit page (/employee/submit) shows a placeholder card with only a logout button, not a full receipt upload form
known_limitations.impact: WF2 receipt upload cannot be tested through this route; may be accessible at a different path or via the employee claims page

known_limitations.issue: KPI cards on HR dashboard (Auto-Approval Rate 84.2%) show hardcoded mock values, not live data
known_limitations.impact: These metrics cannot be validated against actual backend data

known_limitations.issue: Forgot Password feature shows MVP restriction toast — not implemented
known_limitations.impact: Password recovery flow cannot be tested

known_limitations.issue: Edit existing policy is local-only (localStorage) for metadata; only new policy creation calls the real backend upload API
known_limitations.impact: Edits to existing mock policies are not persisted to backend

known_limitations.issue: HR History page shows "History is temporarily restricted" message in MVP
known_limitations.impact: HR history search, claim detail, and export settlement form cannot be tested

known_limitations.issue: Demo employee account has no pre-seeded claims in the database
known_limitations.impact: Employee audit trail and claim detail tests are blocked without prior WF2 submission
